reflections.context: Implement fuzzy phase search to match aim search capabilities, allowing users to find phases even with typos or partial matches.
reflections.outcome: Replaced FlexSearch-only phase search with Fuse.js fuzzy matching. Configured with threshold 0.4 for balanced precision/recall, allowing reasonable typos while avoiding too many false positives. All 129 unit tests pass.
reflections.effectiveness: Using a dedicated fuzzy search library was simpler than trying to tune FlexSearch's context parameters. Fuse.js provides clear threshold configuration and good documentation for fuzzy matching behavior.
reflections.lesson: When implementing fuzzy search, use a library specifically designed for it (Fuse.js) rather than trying to configure a general full-text search library (FlexSearch) to be fuzzy. The specialized library provides clearer controls and more predictable behavior.
reflections.pattern: This follows the pattern of using the right tool for the job: FlexSearch excels at fast full-text indexing, Fuse.js excels at fuzzy matching. Both can coexist in the codebase serving different purposes.

status.comment: Implemented fuzzy phase search using Fuse.js library with threshold 0.4 for better typo tolerance and near-match capabilities.
